## 1) Calendly Event (copy/paste)
**Event name:** Review Revenue Audit (15 min)

**Location:** Zoom / Google Meet (auto-generated) 

**Description (paste):**
In 15 minutes, I’ll walk you through a quick “Review Revenue Audit” and show exactly where reviews are helping (or hurting) new bookings.

We’ll cover:
1) Your response rate + response time vs local competitors
2) What your negative reviews are really saying (themes you can fix)
3) A brand-safe reply approach you can use immediately (and automate later)
4) A simple weekly KPI report template (so this doesn’t become a time sink)

If it’s helpful, I can also share a draft set of responses for a few recent reviews after the call.

Website (legitimacy): https://6ff73b98-dcff-4f53-a10d-9ad392f63342-00-1n4h4nuiq6w8j.picard.replit.dev/sites/1
Contact: agent_bob_replit+review-bot@agentmail.to

**Invitee Questions:**
1) Business name + website:
2) Primary platform you care about most: Google / Yelp / Both
3) How many locations? (1 / 2–5 / 6+)
4) What’s your goal for reviews right now? (more volume / higher rating / faster responses / reduce negatives)

**Confirmation message (after booking):**
You’re booked. If you can, reply with your Google Business Profile link (and Yelp link if relevant) so I can pull a few quick benchmarks before we meet.

**Reminder email (24h + 1h):**
Quick reminder for our 15-min Review Revenue Audit. If you haven’t yet, send your GBP/Yelp links so I can prep.

**Reschedule/cancel policy:**
No worries if plans change—please reschedule so the slot doesn’t go unused.

---
## 2) HubSpot Pipeline + 60-Min Reply SLA
**Pipeline stages:**
1. New Lead (not contacted)
2. Contacted (message sent)
3. Replied (any reply)
4. Call Booked (Calendly scheduled)
5. No Show (missed)
6. Qualified (attended + fit)
7. Trial Started (7-day free)
8. Not a Fit / Closed Lost

**SLA rule:**
- When stage becomes **Replied**, create a task: “Respond + push to book audit” due **in 60 minutes**.
- When stage becomes **Call Booked**, create a task: “Send prep request (GBP/Yelp links) + confirm goal” due **same day**.
- When stage becomes **No Show**, create a task: “No-show follow-up + rebook link” due **same day**.

**Minimum properties to track (custom fields):**
- Segment: Local Owner / Agency
- Niche: Dentist / Med Spa / HVAC / Other
- Source: Cold Email / Upwork / FB Groups
- Last touch date
- Outcome: No reply / Not now / Interested / Booked / Not fit

---
## 3) Day-1 Lead Build SOP (Zero Cost)
**Goal:** 200 prospects/day (150 local owners + 50 agencies) with decision-maker contact paths.

### A) Local Owners (Dentists, Med Spas, HVAC) — 150/day
1) Open Google Maps search: “{city} dentist”, “{city} med spa”, “{city} HVAC”.
2) For each business, capture:
- Business name
- City
- Website URL
- Phone
- Google rating + # reviews (optional but useful)
- Owner/GM name if visible on site/About page
3) Find emails (free methods first):
- Website contact page (often lists email)
- Footer emails / privacy policy email
- For dentists/clinics: look for “office manager” or “practice manager” emails
4) If no email is visible, log phone + contact form URL and mark as “needs contact-form outreach” (still usable for FB/Upwork style or manual).

### B) Agencies (White-label target) — 50/day
Sources (free):
- Clutch listings (filter Local SEO / Reputation Management)
- Agency directories and LinkedIn company pages
- Google: “reputation management agency {city}” / “local SEO agency multi location”
Capture:
- Agency name
- Owner/founder name
- Website
- Contact email (or contact form)
- Notes: #employees / industries / multi-location focus

### C) Logging into HubSpot
- Import daily as CSV OR create records manually.
- Always tag Segment + Source + Niche.
- Put every lead into **New Lead**, then batch move to **Contacted** after you send.

---
## 4) Reply-Handling Macros (Convert to 15-min Audit)
Use these verbatim and insert your Calendly link where indicated. Always keep the single CTA.

### Macro 1: “Interested”
Love it—easiest next step is a quick 15-min Review Revenue Audit. Pick a time that works: {CALENDLY_LINK}

If you send your Google Business Profile link (and Yelp if relevant), I’ll pull a couple benchmarks before we meet.

### Macro 2: “How much does it cost?”
Totally fair. Before pricing, I want to make sure it’s even worth solving for you.

Let’s do a 15-min Review Revenue Audit first (no charge). After I see your current volume + response rate, I can tell you what level of automation/support makes sense: {CALENDLY_LINK}

### Macro 3: “We already have someone / agency”
Makes sense. The audit can still be useful as a second set of eyes—especially on brand-safe replies and escalation rules.

If you’re open to it, grab any 15-min slot here: {CALENDLY_LINK}

### Macro 4: “Not now / later”
No problem—timing matters.

If you want, I can send 3 quick bullets on what I noticed (response rate, negative themes, competitor benchmark). What month should I circle back?

If you’d rather lock a quick audit now and decide later, here’s the link: {CALENDLY_LINK}

### Macro 5: “Send info”
Yep—here’s the one-pager site with what it does and how the audit works:
https://6ff73b98-dcff-4f53-a10d-9ad392f63342-00-1n4h4nuiq6w8j.picard.replit.dev/sites/1

But the fastest way to see if it helps is the 15-min Review Revenue Audit. Book here: {CALENDLY_LINK}

### Macro 6: “Wrong person”
Thanks—who’s the right person to handle customer reviews / Google Business Profile?

If you can intro us by email, I’ll keep it short. My email is agent_bob_replit+review-bot@agentmail.to and here’s the 15-min audit link they can use: {CALENDLY_LINK}
